title: Bill Simmons seems convinced that ‘all signs are pointing to’ this date for Jayson Tatum’s return
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Bill Simmons believes Jayson Tatum will return to play against the Dallas Mavericks this Friday at TD Garden.
The Celtics have not announced an official return date for Tatum, who is recovering from achilles tendon surgery.
Tatum was cleared for 5-on-5 practice, but remains out for Tuesday's game against the Milwaukee Bucks.

title: Should teens be allowed to carry handguns in Massachusetts?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: The Massachusetts Supreme Judicial Court will review a law prohibiting handgun possession for individuals under 21.
The case arises from Mikai Thomson's 2021 conviction for illegal firearm possession, challenging the state's age restriction.
Gun-rights advocates argue the law contravenes the Second Amendment, citing a 2022 Supreme Court decision.

title: 5 takeaways from Don Sweeney’s Bruins press conference before trade deadline
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Don Sweeney plans to explore trade options to strengthen the Bruins before the NHL trade deadline.
The Bruins aim to avoid short-term rental deals, focusing instead on long-term improvements.
Sweeney expresses willingness to acquire high-impact players with secure, long-term contracts.

title: Giannis Antetokounmpo set to return against Celtics while Jaylen Brown is out with an illness
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo is expected to return for the Milwaukee Bucks against the Celtics after missing 15 games.
Celtics guard Jaylen Brown will miss Tuesday's game against the Bucks due to a non-COVID illness.
The Celtics have won eight of the last 10 games and are currently second in the Eastern Conference standings.

title: Citing ‘wokeness,’ Pentagon cancels military fellowships at New England schools like MIT and Tufts
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: The Pentagon is canceling 93 military fellowships at 22 institutions, including MIT, Tufts, and Brown, citing 'leftist ideology.'
Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth claims the programs promote 'anti-American resentment' and sacrifice free expression for 'wokeness.'
New fellowship partners will be sought based on criteria like 'intellectual freedom' and minimal opposition to the Department of Defense.

title: Rescued pocket-sized owl turning heads in Mass.
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: A saw-whet owl was rescued in New England, found injured and unable to fly in a driveway.
Newhouse Wildlife Rescue in Chelmsford is caring for the owl, which has two broken bones and is getting medical exams.
The owl has been transferred to Cape Ann Wildlife; a fundraiser supports the center as its founder battles lung cancer.

title: Massachusetts joins insurers cutting coverage for popular weight-loss drugs
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Massachusetts Group Insurance Commission voted 10-7 to end GLP-1 weight-loss drug coverage for state employees.
The decision affects 22,000 members and aims to save $46 million amid rising drug spending and state budget constraints.
Opponents fear increased health disparities and higher long-term costs; educators urge continued coverage for affordability.

title: After Columbia incident, Healey and Campbell remind schools that ICE needs a judicial warrant to enter dorms
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: ICE agents entered Columbia dorm without warrant, misleadingly claiming a search for a missing child, targeting student Elmina Aghayeva.
Massachusetts Governor Healey and AG Campbell issue guidance reminding schools ICE needs a judicial warrant to access dorms.
The guidance targets public and private institutions, advising schools to designate restricted areas and post warning signs.

title: Lawsuit challenges National Park Service over content removal, including at a Mass. site
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A lawsuit filed in Boston challenges the National Park Service over content removal and alteration under a Trump administration order.
Changes include the removal of labor history films in Lowell, Massachusetts, and other alterations across New England sites.
Massachusetts Senator Ed Markey and Maine Congresswoman Chellie Pingree expressed concerns about historical censorship in national parks.

title: Mass. police dispatcher injured in Iran conflict
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: A Sudbury police dispatcher was injured in a missile strike while serving with U.S. Navy in Dubai.
The missile strikes were part of the U.S.-Israeli conflict with Iran, leading to four American service members' deaths.
The dispatcher's identity remains anonymous for security reasons; he suffered minor injuries and is reported to be fine.
